name: S&P 500
summary: Day 69 Thursday — **TEXTBOOK PEACE-DIVIDEND SESSION: S&P 500 7,365.12 (+1.5% RECORD); NASDAQ 25,838.94 (+2.0%, +17.4% 1M); DOW 49,910.59 (+1.2%); VIX 17.39 (-32.5% 1M); SMH $549.76 (+5.2%, +37.5% 1M); NIKKEI 62,917.61 (+5.7% Golden Week catch-up); KOSPI 7,485.20 (+1.4%, +37.3% 1M); ENERGY XLE -4.1%; FERTILIZERS CF -6.5%/NTR -4.4%; DEFENSE ITA +3.7% BUT AEROSPACE-LED; LOCKHEED $514.26 (-18.1% 1M).** 25 of 62 tracked assets flagged. Single most analytically interesting print: **Nikkei +5.7% (Golden Week reopening absorbing 3 sessions of US AI records in one day; SoftBank +13-16%, Advantest +8%, Tokyo Electron +9%) — international validation of AI-cycle decoupling.** **Critical attribution: ITA +3.7% MASKS underlying split** — ITA's top holdings GE Aerospace (19.4%) + RTX (15.1%) + Boeing (10.3%) > 40% of ETF; Boeing/GE commercial-aero recovery + GE Aerospace $190B order book + $1B US-ops + 5,000 hires drives the print, NOT defense; LMT (pure-play defense) only +1.0% and -18.1% 1M = peace-track is BEARISH for pure defense. Cross-asset peace pricing: Brent collapsed -7% May 6 to ~$103 on Axios MOU scoop; XLE -4.1% on forward-curve repricing despite flat spot; CF/NTR -6.5%/-4.4% on April 17 playbook rerun (Hormuz reopening = fertilizer normalization expectation). Saudi Tadawul -0.5% the **only major equity index in red on global risk-on day** — peace dividend is bearish for war's primary fiscal beneficiary. **VIX 17.39 = cycle-low fear regime — most fragile structural risk in tape:** if Iran rejects MOU within 48hr window, VIX could spike 17 → 28-32 (60-90% expansion plausible from compressed levels). Long VIX calls dated through May 21 (Iran response + signature window) are cleanest tail hedge. Gold +1.1% to $4,732 — 85% dollar-weakness driven (DXY 4-year low, Rieder Fed-chair speculation), 15% war-risk residual. URA +7.5% — the rare asset benefiting from BOTH peace track (HEU custodian path) and structural AI power demand.
